description_long: This awning features a pitched, gabled roof with a reddish-brown shingle covering, supported by a robust timber frame. The structure is held up by two thick wooden posts, each anchored into brick bases for added stability. The roof is constructed with visible wooden trusses and beams, providing both structural integrity and a decorative touch. The open sides and front allow for easy passage beneath, making it suitable for use as a shelter or entryway cover in urban environments. The wood grain and brick textures are clearly defined, giving the awning a natural and sturdy appearance. The overall design is simple yet functional, with no visible signage or decorative trim on the canopy edges.
description: A gabled, shingle-roofed awning with a timber frame and brick base supports, featuring open sides and a sturdy, natural appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open entryway, timber beams, and pitched roof supported by brick-based wooden posts.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full expanse of the shingled roof and the solid wooden frame beneath.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the open back, visible roof trusses, and the symmetry of the supporting structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the shingle pattern and the supporting posts and beams.
attribute_edge_trim_style: straight edge with no decorative trim
attribute_support_structure_type: fixed frame with timber posts and beams
